papers.title: AsyncOPD: How Stale Can On-Policy Distillation Be?
papers.abstract: Abstract Asynchronous on-policy distillation addresses training bottlenecks in large language model post-training by decoupling rollout generation from learner updates, though it introduces challenges with stale policy data that require specialized solutions.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct On-policy distillation (OPD) trains a student on its own rollouts guided by teacher feedback and is becoming increasingly important for large language model (LLM) post-training. Like reinforcement learning (RL), however, OPD faces an on-policy systems bottleneck, as rollouts can dominate training time for reasoning workloads. Asynchronous training pipelines can alleviate this bottleneck by decoupling rollout generation from learner updates, but doing so introduces stale-policy data. While prior work has studied stale data in asynchronous RL, its effects in OPD remain underexplored. We present the first systematic study of staleness in asynchronous OPD, focusing on a practical setting where teacher feedback is implemented through local KL losses and full-vocabulary teacher logits are too expensive to store or transfer, necessitating finite teacher-score caches. We first show that KL direction changes the stale-data problem: teacher-weighted forward KL is more robust to stale rollouts, whereas student-weighted reverse KL is vulnerable. Second, for this vulnerable reverse-KL case, we study whether methods designed to stabilize asynchronous RL can mitigate OPD staleness. In our experiments, they do not improve over a simpler OPD-specific surrogate: recomputing the reverse-KL signal under the current student at learner time. Third, we analyze how finite teacher-score caches create a bias-variance tradeoff for sparse and sampled reverse-KL OPD estimators. This motivates multi-sample Monte Carlo (MC), which preserves MC correctability while reducing one-sample variance. Finally, we present and open-source AsyncOPD, a fully asynchronous OPD training pipeline built from these estimator choices. Experiments show that AsyncOPD improves training throughput by 1.6times to 3.8times over strict synchronous training while reaching comparable accuracy.

ru.title: Асинхронная дистилляция для быстрого обучения больших языковых моделей
ru.desc: В статье исследуется асинхронная дистилляция моделей на собственных данных (on-policy distillation), которая ускоряет обучение больших языковых моделей путем разделения процесса генерации примеров и обновления весов модели. Авторы показывают, что использование обратной KL дивергенции более чувствительно к устаревшим данным, чем прямая KL, и предлагают перевычисление сигнала потерь под текущей моделью во время обучения. Для эффективной работы с ограниченным кэшем оценок учителя предложен метод многовыборочного Монте-Карло, который снижает дисперсию оценок. Разработанная система AsyncOPD показывает ускорение обучения в 1.6-3.8 раза при сохранении качества модели.

en.title: Boosting Training Efficiency with AsyncOPD
en.desc: This paper introduces Asynchronous On-Policy Distillation (OPD) to improve the efficiency of training large language models by separating the generation of rollouts from the updates to the learner. It addresses the issue of stale policy data that arises from this decoupling, which can negatively impact training performance. The authors conduct a systematic study on how stale data affects OPD, particularly focusing on the differences between teacher-weighted and student-weighted KL divergence. They propose a new training pipeline, AsyncOPD, which enhances training speed significantly while maintaining accuracy, demonstrating the effectiveness of their approach in handling the challenges of asynchronous training.

zh.title: 异步在线蒸馏：提升大型语言模型训练效率的创新方法
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了异步在线蒸馏（Asynchronous On-Policy Distillation）在大型语言模型后训练中的应用，旨在解决训练瓶颈问题。通过将回滚生成与学习者更新解耦，异步训练管道可以缓解这一瓶颈，但也引入了过时策略数据的问题。研究表明，教师加权的前向KL散度对过时回滚更具鲁棒性，而学生加权的反向KL则容易受到影响。最后，论文提出了AsyncOPD，一个完全异步的在线蒸馏训练管道，实验结果显示其训练效率提高了1.6到3.8倍，同时保持了相似的准确性。

papers.title: Cognitive Episodes in LLM Reasoning Traces Enable Interpretable Human Item Difficulty Prediction
papers.abstract: Abstract Epi2Diff framework transforms LRM reasoning traces into cognitive episodes to predict human item difficulty more accurately than existing methods.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Predicting human item difficulty is central to educational assessment, where reliable estimates support fairness and effective test construction. Existing methods often depend on costly human calibration or item-level textual representations, providing limited evidence about the cognitive processes that make items difficult. We argue that difficulty should be viewed not only as a property of item text, but also as an observable consequence of the problem-solving burden an item induces. Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) offer scalable process evidence through reasoning traces, but such evidence must be structured to support interpretable modeling. To this end, we introduce Epi2Diff (Episode to Difficulty), a framework that maps LRM reasoning traces into cognitively grounded episode sequences. These episodes group trace segments into functional problem-solving states, enabling difficulty to be modeled through reasoning scale, effort allocation, and state transitions. Epi2Diff extracts compact episode-dynamic features and combines them with semantic item representations for human difficulty prediction. Experiments on four real-world human difficulty datasets show that Epi2Diff consistently outperforms strong baselines, including fine-tuned small language models, LLM in-context learning, and supervised LLM adaptation. On SAT-derived classification benchmarks, Epi2Diff achieves an 8.1% average relative gain over supervised LLM fine-tuning baselines. Further analyses show that harder items induce more effortful, iterative, and implementation-centered episode dynamics, rather than merely longer responses. These results demonstrate that cognitive episodes in LRM reasoning traces provide a predictive and interpretable process representation for human item difficulty, offering a new lens for educational measurement with reasoning models.

ru.title: Когнитивные эпизоды рассуждений для предсказания сложности тестовых заданий
ru.desc: В статье представлена фреймворк Epi2Diff, которая преобразует трассы рассуждений больших моделей рассуждений в когнитивные эпизоды для более точного предсказания сложности заданий для человека. Авторы утверждают, что сложность задания следует рассматривать не только как свойство текста, но и как следствие когнитивной нагрузки при решении задачи. Фреймворк преобразует сегменты трассы рассуждений в функциональные состояния решения проблем и извлекает признаки динамики эпизодов для моделирования сложности через масштаб рассуждений и распределение усилий. Эксперименты на четырёх наборах данных показывают, что Epi2Diff превосходит базовые методы, включая fine-tuned LLM и LLM in-context learning, с улучшением на 8.1% в среднем.

en.title: Transforming Reasoning into Predictive Cognitive Episodes
en.desc: The Epi2Diff framework enhances the prediction of human item difficulty in educational assessments by transforming reasoning traces from Large Reasoning Models (LRMs) into cognitive episodes. This approach allows for a deeper understanding of the problem-solving processes that contribute to item difficulty, moving beyond traditional methods that rely on item text alone. By structuring reasoning traces into functional problem-solving states, Epi2Diff captures essential features related to reasoning scale and effort allocation. Experimental results demonstrate that Epi2Diff significantly outperforms existing models, providing a more interpretable and effective means of assessing item difficulty.

zh.title: Epi2Diff：精准预测人类项目难度的新方法
zh.desc: Epi2Diff框架将大型推理模型（LRM）的推理轨迹转化为认知情节，以更准确地预测人类项目的难度。该方法不仅考虑项目文本的特性，还关注解决问题所带来的认知负担。通过将推理轨迹结构化为功能性问题解决状态，Epi2Diff能够提取紧凑的情节动态特征，并与语义项目表示结合，进行人类难度预测。实验结果表明，Epi2Diff在多个真实世界数据集上表现优于现有的强基线方法。

papers.title: MIMFlow: Integrating Masked Image Modeling with Normalizing Flows for End-to-End Image Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract MIMFlow combines Normalizing Flows with Masked Image Modeling to improve generative modeling by decoupling semantic representation from pixel-level details, achieving better performance with fewer tokens.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Normalizing Flows (NFs) are powerful generative models capable of exact density estimation and sampling. However, their strict invertibility often forces the model to exhaust its capacity on low-level pixel details, hindering the capture of high-level semantic structures. While Masked Image Modeling (MIM) has excelled in representation learning, its integration into generative pipelines has remained largely modular and disjointed. In this paper, we propose MIMFlow, a unified end-to-end framework that jointly optimizes latent semantics, pixel reconstruction, and generative flow. By employing a VAE encoder to infer semantic latent from masked images, MIMFlow achieves a principled decoupling of the generative task: the Normalizing Flow focuses on modeling a simplified, low-frequency semantic manifold, while a specialized decoder handles high-frequency synthesis. This design effectively resolves the inherent capacity bottleneck of NFs, allowing the model to prioritize global structural coherence over redundant noise. Empirical results on ImageNet 256times256 show that MIMFlow-L reaches 71.3\% linear probing accuracy and an FID of 2.50. Despite using only 128 tokens (50\% fewer than standard models), it yields a 32.8\% performance gain over similar-scale NF baselines. Our code is available at https://github.com/MCG-NJU/MIMFlow.
affiliations: State Key Laboratory for Novel Software Technology, Nanjing University

ru.title: Разделение семантики и деталей в генеративном моделировании через объединённый поток
ru.desc: MIMFlow объединяет нормализующие потоки (Normalizing Flows) с маскированным моделированием изображений (Masked Image Modeling) для улучшения генеративного моделирования. Основная идея заключается в разделении задачи генерации: нормализующий поток фокусируется на моделировании семантического многообразия низких частот, а специализированный декодер отвечает за синтез высоких частот. Такой подход преодолевает узкое место пропускной способности нормализующих потоков, позволяя модели сосредоточиться на глобальной структурной согласованности. Модель достигает впечатляющих результатов на ImageNet 256×256, используя на 50% меньше токенов, чем стандартные подходы.

en.title: Decoupling Semantics from Pixels for Better Generative Modeling
en.desc: MIMFlow is a novel framework that enhances generative modeling by integrating Normalizing Flows with Masked Image Modeling. It effectively separates the representation of high-level semantics from low-level pixel details, allowing for improved performance with fewer tokens. By using a Variational Autoencoder (VAE) to extract semantic information from masked images, MIMFlow enables the Normalizing Flow to focus on capturing essential global structures rather than getting bogged down by noise. This approach results in significant performance improvements, as demonstrated by its high accuracy and low Fréchet Inception Distance (FID) on the ImageNet dataset.

zh.title: MIMFlow：解耦语义与细节的生成建模新方法
zh.desc: MIMFlow结合了归一化流和掩码图像建模，以改善生成建模。它通过将语义表示与像素级细节解耦，使用更少的标记实现了更好的性能。该框架采用变分自编码器（VAE）从掩码图像中推断语义潜变量，使得生成任务的解耦更加合理。实验结果表明，MIMFlow在性能上显著优于传统模型，展示了其在生成建模中的潜力。

papers.title: Bridging VideoQA and Video-Guided Agentic Tasks via Generalized Keyframe Extraction
papers.abstract: Abstract A new benchmark evaluates multimodal large language models' ability to understand video content and perform GUI tasks, while a novel keyframe extraction method improves performance on both video question answering and video-guided agentic tasks.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Video understanding is a fundamental capability for multimodal intelligence, and recent Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) have achieved remarkable performance on Video Question Answering (VideoQA) benchmarks. However, existing benchmarks primarily evaluate whether models can perceive shallow visual cues, while rarely examining whether MLLMs can learn deeper knowledge or procedural skills from video tutorials and generalize them to downstream long-horizon agentic tasks. To address this gap, we introduce VG-GUIBench (Video-Guided GUI Benchmark), a new benchmark designed to evaluate whether MLLM-based GUI agents can follow video tutorials to complete corresponding GUI interactive tasks. Furthermore, we observe that the performance of models on both VideoQA and video-guided agentic tasks critically depends on effective keyframe extraction. Based on this observation, we propose TASKER (Task-driven And Scene-aware Keyframe searchER), a keyframe extraction algorithm that jointly considers task relevance and scene dynamics to identify informative frames. Experimental results demonstrate that TASKER achieves significant performance improvements on both VideoQA and video-guided agentic task benchmarks, outperforming the best baseline by 2.0% on the EgoSchema fullset and 1.8% on the NExT-QA dataset, respectively. These results further highlight the potential of generalized keyframe extraction methods for video understanding tasks. Our code and data are available at https://github.com/VG-GUI-TASKER/VG-GUI-TASKER.

ru.title: Умное выделение ключевых кадров для понимания видео и выполнения GUI задач
ru.desc: В работе представлен новый бенчмарк VG-GUIBench для оценки способности мультимодальных больших языковых моделей следовать видеоинструкциям и выполнять задачи взаимодействия с графическим интерфейсом. Авторы предлагают метод TASKER для выделения ключевых кадров из видео, который учитывает релевантность задачи и динамику сцены одновременно. Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что предложенный подход к извлечению ключевых кадров улучшает производительность как на задачах видео-вопросно-ответного тестирования, так и на задачах агентов, управляемых видео. Работа демонстрирует, что качественное выделение информативных кадров критически влияет на способность моделей понимать видеоконтент и применять полученные знания для выполнения долгосрочных задач.

en.title: Enhancing Video Understanding with TASKER and VG-GUIBench
en.desc: This paper introduces VG-GUIBench, a new benchmark for evaluating multimodal large language models (MLLMs) on their ability to understand video content and perform graphical user interface (GUI) tasks. It highlights the limitations of existing benchmarks that focus only on shallow visual cues, emphasizing the need for models to learn deeper knowledge and procedural skills from video tutorials. The authors propose TASKER, a novel keyframe extraction method that enhances performance in both video question answering (VideoQA) and video-guided tasks by selecting frames based on task relevance and scene dynamics. Experimental results show that TASKER significantly improves model performance, indicating the importance of effective keyframe extraction in video understanding.

zh.title: 提升视频理解能力的关键帧提取方法
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一个新的基准测试，评估多模态大型语言模型（MLLMs）理解视频内容和执行图形用户界面（GUI）任务的能力。研究者们提出了一种新的关键帧提取方法，显著提高了视频问答（VideoQA）和视频引导的代理任务的性能。通过引入VG-GUIBench基准，研究者们能够评估MLLMs在完成GUI交互任务时，是否能够有效地跟随视频教程。实验结果表明，TASKER算法在关键帧提取方面的创新，能够显著提升模型在相关任务上的表现。

papers.title: How Good Can Linear Models Be for Time-Series Forecasting?
papers.abstract: Abstract Research demonstrates that preprocessing optimizations, particularly in context length, normalization, and regularization, can significantly improve time-series forecasting accuracy more effectively than scaling model architectures.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Time-series forecasting research has been moving steadily toward larger architectures, from specialized transformers to general-purpose foundation models, on the assumption that capacity is what unlocks accuracy. We take the opposite position: most of the gap can be closed at far lower cost by tuning preprocessing rather than scaling models. We use Ridge regression as the testbed, since it has a closed-form solution and interpretable weights, which let the optimal hyperparameters be read off the search directly. We search over context length, local normalization, regularization, and augmentation on eight standard benchmarks and find three patterns. (1) Optimal lookback is strongly series-specific and often non-monotonic in forecast horizon, with fitted power-law exponents ranging from +0.46 on ETTm2 to -0.19 on Exchange and Traffic, challenging the convention that longer horizons need longer history. (2) Normalizing over a learned trailing fraction of the context, rather than its entirety, is almost universally preferred. (3) Series within the same dataset often disagree on hyperparameters; the optimal degree of cross-series sharing varies from fully shared to fully per-series. The resulting models beat prior linear forecasters on most dataset-horizon entries and exceed Transformer, MLP, and CNN baselines on six of eight benchmarks. The optimized hyperparameters also serve as a diagnostic on the data itself, revealing structures that larger models absorb silently into their learned parameters.

ru.title: Предварительная обработка важнее масштаба: оптимизация гиперпараметров для прогнозирования временных рядов
ru.desc: В статье исследуется, как оптимизация предварительной обработки данных, включая длину контекста, нормализацию и регуляризацию, может значительно улучшить качество прогнозирования временных рядов без увеличения архитектуры модели. Авторы используют гребневую регрессию как простой и интерпретируемый инструмент для поиска оптимальных гиперпараметров на восьми стандартных бенчмарках. Они обнаруживают, что оптимальная длина истории сильно зависит от конкретного ряда и часто немонотонна относительно горизонта прогноза, а нормализация по части контекста предпочтительнее полной нормализации. Оптимизированные модели превосходят как линейные методы, так и сложные архитектуры (трансформеры, MLP, CNN), демонстрируя, что качество часто определяется правильной подготовкой данных, а не масштабом сети.

en.title: Optimize Preprocessing, Not Just Model Size!
en.desc: This paper argues that improving time-series forecasting accuracy can be achieved more effectively through preprocessing optimizations rather than simply increasing model size. The authors focus on tuning aspects like context length, normalization, and regularization using Ridge regression, which allows for clear interpretation of hyperparameters. They identify three key patterns: optimal lookback varies by series and forecast horizon, normalization over a learned fraction of context is preferred, and hyperparameters differ significantly across series within the same dataset. Their findings show that these optimized models outperform traditional linear forecasters and even some advanced architectures like Transformers and CNNs on multiple benchmarks.

zh.title: 优化预处理，提升时间序列预测准确性
zh.desc: 本研究表明，预处理优化，特别是在上下文长度、归一化和正则化方面，可以显著提高时间序列预测的准确性，效果优于单纯扩大模型架构。我们采用岭回归作为测试平台，因为它具有封闭形式的解和可解释的权重，便于直接读取最佳超参数。研究发现，最佳的回溯长度因序列而异，并且在预测范围内通常是非单调的，挑战了更长的预测范围需要更长历史的传统观念。此外，针对同一数据集的序列，最佳超参数的共享程度差异很大，优化后的超参数还可以作为数据本身的诊断工具，揭示出大型模型在学习参数中默默吸收的结构。

papers.title: GUICrafter: Weakly-Supervised GUI Agent Leveraging Massive Unannotated Screenshots
papers.abstract: Abstract GUICrafter addresses GUI agent data challenges through a weakly-supervised approach using unannotated screenshots and a two-stage curriculum learning framework for visual grounding and reinforcement learning calibration.  Generated by Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-32B-Instruct Data, as the fundamental substrate of modern intelligence, has greatly driven the development of current foundation models. Naturally, researchers aim to extend this paradigm to the domain of GUI agents, hoping to build strong GUI agents through a similar paradigm. However, GUI agent data cannot be directly harvested from the internet, making it costly and difficult to collect at scale. As a result, current GUI agents suffer from poor cross-device generalization and limited visual grounding ability for fine-grained GUI elements. As an attempt to address data challenge in GUI agents, we propose GUICrafter, a weakly-supervised GUI agent leveraging massive unannotated screenshots to substantially reduce the reliance on expensive human annotations. GUICrafter explores a curriculum learning framework for training GUI agents through two progressive stages. First, the model learns visual grounding from large-scale unannotated screenshots and webpages, leveraging the rich contextual signals inherent in GUI interactions without human annotations. Then, in Stage 2, we leverage a small amount of high-quality data to calibrate the model via reinforcement learning. Experiments show that GUICrafter achieves competitive, or even superior, performance to advanced systems like UI-TARS while using only 0.1% of its data. Furthermore, under the same amount of annotated data, GUICrafter surpasses all previous methods such as GUI-R1. Code, data, and models are available at https://github.com/fansunqi/GUICrafter.

ru.title: Слабо-контролируемое обучение GUI агентов через curriculum learning
ru.desc: GUICrafter решает проблему недостатка данных для обучения GUI агентов, используя слабо-контролируемый подход с неразмеченными скриншотами. Модель использует двухэтапное curriculum learning: на первом этапе агент учится визуальному пониманию элементов интерфейса из больших объемов неразмеченных данных, на втором этапе проводится калибровка с помощью reinforcement learning на небольшом наборе качественных аннотаций. Метод достигает сравнимых или лучших результатов, чем существующие системы, используя всего 0.1% от требуемого объема размеченных данных. Подход значительно улучшает обобщение модели на разных устройствах и повышает точность распознавания элементов интерфейса.

en.title: Empowering GUI Agents with Weakly-Supervised Learning
en.desc: GUICrafter is a novel approach to developing GUI agents that addresses the challenges of data scarcity by utilizing a weakly-supervised learning method. It employs unannotated screenshots to train the model in two stages: first, it learns visual grounding from these images, and then it fine-tunes the model using a small set of high-quality annotated data through reinforcement learning. This method significantly reduces the need for expensive human annotations while improving the model's ability to generalize across different devices and accurately identify GUI elements. Experimental results demonstrate that GUICrafter outperforms existing systems with minimal data, showcasing its effectiveness in the field of GUI agent development.

zh.title: GUICrafter：高效构建图形用户界面代理的创新方法
zh.desc: GUICrafter是一种弱监督的图形用户界面（GUI）代理，旨在解决GUI代理数据的挑战。它利用大量未标注的截图，通过两阶段的课程学习框架进行视觉定位和强化学习校准。第一阶段，模型从未标注的截图和网页中学习视觉定位，利用GUI交互中的丰富上下文信号。第二阶段，使用少量高质量数据通过强化学习对模型进行校准，实验表明GUICrafter在数据使用效率上优于现有的先进系统。
